skill.name: skill-developer
skill.description: Create and manage Claude Code skills following Anthropic best practices. Use when creating new skills, modifying skill-rules.json, understanding trigger patterns, working with hooks, debugging skill activation issues.
skill.summary: Guide for creating and managing Claude Code skills with auto-activation system, triggers, hooks, and best practices.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: This is a documentation-only skill containing markdown guides for creating Claude Code skills. The static scanner flagged patterns in documentation text (markdown code blocks, regex examples, keywords) as security issues. All findings are false positives - there is no executable code, no user input handling, and no malicious intent. The skill provides legitimate educational content about skill development.

medium_findings.title: Ruby/Shell Backtick Execution (False Positive)
medium_findings.description: Scanner detected backtick syntax in markdown code blocks showing example commands. These are documentation examples, not actual code execution. Example: `echo 'test' | npx tsx` is a documentation example, not shell execution.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: These are markdown documentation files containing code examples in backtick-marked code blocks. The backticks are markdown syntax for code formatting, not Ruby/shell execution operators.

medium_findings.title: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm (False Positive)
medium_findings.description: Scanner detected regex patterns in documentation as cryptographic algorithms. Documentation shows regex examples like `(create|add).*?(table|database)` for skill trigger patterns.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: These are regex patterns in documentation explaining how to configure skill triggers. Regex is used for pattern matching in skill configuration, not cryptography.

medium_findings.title: Hardcoded URL (False Positive)
medium_findings.description: Scanner detected URLs in documentation text. URLs like regex101.com appear in troubleshooting sections explaining how to test regex patterns.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: These are static URLs in documentation referring users to external regex testing tools. No network requests are made by this skill.

medium_findings.title: Certificate/Key Files (False Positive)
medium_findings.description: Scanner detected 'keywords' in skill-rules.json configuration reference and flagged it as certificate access.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The word 'keywords' refers to skill trigger configuration (promptTriggers.keywords), not cryptographic keys or certificates.

medium_findings.title: System Reconnaissance (False Positive)
medium_findings.description: Scanner flagged documentation showing how to test hooks manually using echo and cat commands.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: These are documentation examples showing how to manually test skill hooks with sample JSON input. This is legitimate troubleshooting guidance, not reconnaissance.

medium_findings.title: Critical Heuristic: Code Execution + Network + Credentials (False Positive)
medium_findings.description: Scanner combined multiple documentation patterns to create a heuristic finding. The combination is from showing example commands (code blocks), mentioning URLs (documentation), and referencing configuration (keywords).
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: This heuristic incorrectly combines separate documentation elements. There is no actual code execution, network exfiltration, or credential access in this documentation-only skill.

content.value_statement: Learn to build Claude Code skills with triggers, hooks, and best practices. This skill provides comprehensive documentation for creating auto-activating skills that enhance Claude's capabilities.
actual_capabilities: Create new Claude Code skills with proper YAML frontmatter and structure
Configure skill triggers using keywords, intent patterns, file paths, and content detection
Set up hook mechanisms (UserPromptSubmit, Stop hooks) for skill activation
Follow Anthropic best practices including the 500-line rule and progressive disclosure
Debug skill activation issues and test hooks manually
Configure enforcement levels (block, suggest, warn) for skills
limitations: This is a documentation skill only - it provides guidance but does not execute code
Requires manual creation of skill files in the .claude/skills directory
Does not include pre-built skill templates or code generation
Skill activation depends on Claude Code's trigger matching system

use_cases.title: Building Your First Claude Code Skill
use_cases.description: Learn the fundamentals of skill creation including file structure, YAML frontmatter, and trigger configuration.
use_cases.target_user: New users wanting to extend Claude Code capabilities

use_cases.title: Configuring Advanced Skill Triggers
use_cases.description: Master intent patterns, regex-based triggers, file path matching, and content detection for precise skill activation.

use_cases.title: Setting Up Skill Hooks
use_cases.description: Implement UserPromptSubmit and Stop hooks for proactive suggestions and error handling reminders.

prompt_templates.title: Getting Started with Skills
prompt_templates.prompt: How do I create a new Claude Code skill? What is the basic file structure and YAML frontmatter needed?
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner wanting to create their first skill

prompt_templates.title: Understanding Trigger Types
prompt_templates.prompt: Explain the different trigger types for skills: keywords, intent patterns, file paths, and content detection. Give examples of each.

prompt_templates.title: Hook Implementation Guide
prompt_templates.prompt: How do I set up a UserPromptSubmit hook to suggest relevant skills based on user input? Show an example hook implementation.

prompt_templates.title: Debugging Skill Activation
prompt_templates.prompt: My skill is not activating when it should. How do I debug skill trigger issues and test my configuration?

output_examples.input: How do I create a new Claude Code skill?
output_examples.output:
To create a new Claude Code skill:

1. Create a directory in `.claude/skills/{skill-name}/`
2. Add a `SKILL.md` file with YAML frontmatter:
```markdown
---
name: my-skill
description: Brief description with trigger keywords
---

# My Skill

Your skill content here.
```
3. Add entry to `.claude/skills/skill-rules.json`
4. Keep SKILL.md under 500 lines following the 500-line rule.

output_examples.input: What are the different trigger types?
output_examples.output:
Claude Code skills support four trigger types:

1. **Keywords** - Substring matching in prompts (case-insensitive)
2. **Intent Patterns** - Regex patterns for action detection
3. **File Paths** - Glob patterns for location-based activation
4. **Content Detection** - Regex in file contents

Each type serves different activation scenarios.

best_practices: Follow the 500-line rule: keep SKILL.md under 500 lines and use reference files for detailed content
Use progressive disclosure: provide overview in SKILL.md with links to detailed reference documents
Include comprehensive trigger keywords: add all relevant terms, file types, and use cases in the description
Test triggers before publishing: verify skill activates correctly with various prompt patterns
anti_patterns: Do not create skills over 500 lines - use reference files instead to maintain readability
Avoid overly generic keywords that may cause unintended activations
Do not use blocking enforcement (block level) for non-critical skills
Avoid deeply nested reference structures - keep references to one level deep

faq.question: What is the 500-line rule?
faq.answer: The 500-line rule is an Anthropic best practice recommending keeping SKILL.md files under 500 lines. Use reference files (like ADVANCED.md, TROUBLESHOOTING.md) for detailed content beyond this limit.

faq.question: How do skill triggers work?
faq.answer: Skill triggers use four mechanisms: keywords (substring match), intent patterns (regex), file paths (glob patterns), and content detection (regex in files). They are configured in skill-rules.json.

faq.question: What are skill hooks?
faq.answer: Hooks are TypeScript files in .claude/hooks/ that run at specific times. UserPromptSubmit runs before seeing user input (for suggestions), Stop runs after responses (for reminders).

faq.question: What enforcement levels are available?
faq.answer: Three enforcement levels: BLOCK (exits with code 2, use sparingly for critical errors), SUGGEST (injects context, most common), and WARN (advisory notices).

faq.question: How do I test my skill triggers?
faq.answer: Use manual testing commands shown in TROUBLESHOOTING.md: echo JSON payload | npx tsx .claude/hooks/skill-activation-prompt.ts. Also test regex patterns at regex101.com.

faq.question: What is progressive disclosure?
faq.answer: Progressive disclosure is a design pattern where you provide essential info in SKILL.md (<500 lines) and link to detailed reference files for users who need more depth.
